key_insight: Compass improves prostate cancer detection by analyzing micro-ultrasound as a multi-view video sequence rather than isolated single images.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help align AI assessment with how expert readers interpret full micro-ultrasound studies, potentially improving biopsy targeting and diagnostic support.
method: Models continuous rotational sweep videos and biopsy-time frames as a stream of 2D images
Uses a transformer conditioned on probe rotational angle to aggregate evidence across the study
Outputs both frame-level and study-level risk scores
evidence_signal: The model was trained and evaluated on a multi-center clinical trial dataset of micro-ultrasound studies and compared against literature baselines and expert risk scores.
watch_item: Performance depends on multi-center video-quality consistency and accurate angle-aware sequence capture, which may affect real-world implementation.
paper_title: Compass: Prostate Cancer Detection Needs Multi-View Context
